title: e2e_testnet: BOB_SCAN_SECS reused for unrelated scan in testnet_scan_advances_tip
description: wallet/tests/e2e_testnet.rs line 275: testnet_scan_advances_tip uses BOB_SCAN_SECS as the timeout for its own scan_to_tip call. BOB_SCAN_SECS is defined as the time budget for Bob's post-payment scan in testnet_shielded_payment_end_to_end. The connectivity test and the payment test scan are different operations with different characteristics: the connectivity test scans only 5 blocks (fast), while Bob's payment scan may need to process many more blocks. Sharing the constant means you cannot tune one timeout independently of the other. If BOB_SCAN_SECS is reduced for the payment test because the scanner gets faster, the connectivity test silently inherits a tighter budget it may not need; and vice versa. Fix: introduce a separate CONNECTIVITY_SCAN_SECS constant with its own doc comment.
close_reason: P3 bikeshed: both uses share the same reasonable 2-minute budget for scanning a small block window; separate constants add complexity without benefit
